competitors.aiSummary:
# Kamino Summary

Kamino expanded lending markets with new tokenized asset support ($CASH, USCC, FWDI) and incentivized products (Solstice Market: 80% LTV, 1,000 USDC/4,700 USX weekly rewards). PRIME market leads RWA lending with $570M TVL, $250M borrowed across 3,653 loans. Sentora PYUSD Vault ($400M deposits) achieved 8.3% supply APY.

tweets.fullText:
$CASH is now live in the @SuperstateInc Market on Kamino.

With USCC and FWDI, tokenized assets become productive collateral across credit markets on @solana, unlocking liquidity, enabling stablecoin borrowing, and expanding capital efficiency onchain. https://t.co/l0BKweAAmv
tweets.aiAnalysis:
# Why This Tweet Underperformed

The tweet's low engagement (~0.1% interaction rate despite 22K views) reflects niche, announcement-style content that lacks emotional hooks or broad relevance—it's technical product news targeting only existing Kamino/Solana users rather than a wider audience. The heavy jargon (tokenized assets, productive collateral, capital efficiency) and lack of visual content, concrete metrics, or novelty angle failed to create urgency or curiosity beyond the protocol's immediate community.

The "how-to" format and self-promotional nature (promoting own protocol) inherently limits organic reach compared to educational or contrarian takes, and the modest engagement numbers suggest it resonated primarily with existing followers rather than breaking through to broader DeFi audiences.

The tweet underperformed because it **lacks emotional urgency and relies too heavily on niche crypto/DeFi metrics** that only appeal to existing protocol users rather than a broader audience.
